text: "It's a boy!"

title: Cum nox venerit sol poterit oriri
text:
Cum nox venerit sol poterit oriri

Possibilities:
Because the night has come, the sun will be able to rise.
After the night has come, the sun will be able to rise.

Syntax of venerit:
Future perfect third singular active indicative
Perfect third singular active subjunctive

title: Fuck Men
text:
I'm sure I'll end up reading this and saying to myself, "Oh ew, delete that. It's bad writing and it's not good." I'll end up scratching at it and wishing it was written differently, or maybe I'll forget about it and never touch this again. But whatever.

Fuck. Men. I say that a lot. Quite honestly, I wish that those words carried only the meaning of that phrase. Rather than remaining solely as a statement, the phrase "Fuck men" has taken on so many forms for me. Sometimes it is in a fit of anger, where I seek to simply blame my problems on this indescribable force of nature: an ego crushing force shoved into the body of a man. But in other situations it is a way to refer to the unfairness of the world, and all of the cruelty that is so nicely summarized in a transaction of blame. No longer is the world that I experience my fault. It is the men. And yet I am one.

One would think logically that by hating men and vowing never to love them, I would be in a realm of self hatred and loathing as a man myself. But rather what happens is a state of confusion, neither hate nor love, and a deathly desire for someone to show me what it is to "be a man". The phrase fuck men then turns into another meaning all entirely, shifting to express the romantic frustrations of a little gay boy. It begins to represent not only the frustrations of femininity, but the dangerous intersection between manhood and homosexuality. No longer are you just a boy. You are thrust into a world where you are not welcome in the growling den of over-masculinized boys, and neither welcome in the coven of women who do not see you as one of their own. Scrambling, you tumble around, and with every time that you wonder if that guy you like is gay, you forcibly learn that this path is the only one for you. You are, in all senses of the word, lost.

As such, it is not in the absence of a father figure, but instead in the absence of clarity, due to a constantly fluid sense of masculinity, that destroys my right to manhood and gives me the title of "daddy issues". A title that belongs to many people, fictional or not. For one, there is Little Dog. He was much like me, and showed his femininity before expressing the totality of his masculinity. Of course, he differed in his usage of heroin and cocaine, and in the continuous traumatic experience that he called his life. But throughout all of the imbalance and chaos that ensues in his life, he finds that submitting to another man brings him power. Being submissive brings you control. It is a reclamation in a world where your rights to emotion, love, respect, and identity are forcefully snatched from you. Submitting puts that world in your fingertips again, if not only for a brief moment. You are in control of another man's pleasure, and he would not be able to enjoy himself, if not for your generosity and mercy. Because fuck men.

I no longer think back at my ex and cry. I think back now and laugh. Not at the innocence and naivety that young Jasper exuded, but at this dumbfuckery that he put me through. I laugh at the pain, wincing at each tear, but always managing to leave looking tired and haggedy. Perhaps the meaning of age is the progression of your emotional maturity, and the moment you are old is the moment you can wince at pain instead of cowardly running. Perhaps it is that moment where you change from a boy to a man, a girl to a woman. But if I am neither, what do I become?

title: From the diary of a depressed teenager
text:
Dear Irelia,

Depression hit me like a bus today. I had the whole package; suicidal ideation, self harm intent, anxiety, self loathing, and symptoms I swear I could attribute to psychosis. And I can't take it anymore. It's like having freedom yanked from my grasp. It is as if my life is being sucked away. Imagine a circle with a decreasing radius; it is accelerating. Meters become centimeters become millimeters becomes zero: death. What is wrong with me?

Jasper

title: FLOWER
text:
FLOWER

A Blossom
Pops out
Oh no a possum!
Well so much for
that

HEY!
ANOTHER!

By: Jasper Cheng.

I was 7 years old when I started poems. At first it was hard. Then I learned it takes a spirit to make a poem and a rhyme. Then it became easy and I love it.

-10 year old Jasper.

title: I remember
text:
I remember when I didn't have to write in full sentences.
I remember when I wrote tales of dragons and proceeded to write myself into a fantasy.
I remember when the only way I could fall asleep was by imagining myself a dragonrider.
I remember being pummeled.
I remember the concrete ground, surrounded by fields of grass and a playground, where I was imprisoned at Parkway Elementary School.
I remember the blue eyes that matched the white skin of the teacher who watched it happen.
I remember the horror that she refused to show and the action that she never took to save me.
I remember my bedroom, with a chalk drawing of Serperior, the grass snake pokemon, drawn on the wall.
I remember the stuffed animals that kept me safe.
I remember the hot pain and nausea that followed. I remember their names.
I remember the blond hair of Connor, and the knee of dark eyed Marco. I remember Asher as I saw him in the back.
I remember Reva, the girl who stole my first kiss. I remember being forcibly tugged in, even if it was just an innocent smooch.
I remember the first time a man- no, boy, kissed me.
I remember the first time I had sex.
I remember that hot feeling and the lust that felt so eerily similar to the days on the concrete.
I remember the feeling of being pinned down.
I remember the joy and the ecstasy that came with being in his arms.
I remember the excruciating pain, like having my torso ripped open, of heartbreak, and his arms holding me then too.
I remember my sister's princess dress. It was pink and had Cinderella shoes to match it. I remember the flight of stairs I fell down while in them.
I remember the lyrics to Lady Gaga's "Telephone", and of every song by the Spice Girls.
I remember the buildings I walked into while belting those songs. But back then it was cute: because I was 3.
I remember Crae's office. I remember the toys and the jungle gym of blankets that only an occupational therapist would have.
I remember being allowed to chew gum in class.
I remember feeling crazy. But I don't remember when it started.
I remember sitting at the windowsill, wondering what the world beyond would bring me soon.
I remember having ¾ of my body out of a window, hanging by the smallest parts of my body.
I remember not wanting to die.
I remember when my parents hid the knives from me.
I remember when they took my doors' locks.
I remember when they bolted the windows shut.
I remember feeling imprisoned again.
I remember not being able to live without escaping to another world.
I remember when I was happy to be

title: Dear Mildred
text:
Dear Mildred,

I hope that you find peace and silence in a land of uncertainty and mystery. I wish you rest and release from the bonds of the government, the restrictions of human existence, and the misery and dread that accompany them. I hope that you will forgive the tears that are yet to be cried for you and for the world we used to live in. The world where we could ignorantly live in bliss, turning a blind eye to the troubles and small fractures of reality that were hidden in the walls of the parlor and society. I hope that you can forgive the world for tricking you into such a feeble lie; that our world was happy. I hope that you can forgive me for tearing down those walls and exposing us to reality. I hope that you can find peace in unspoken Gods, and that you can find peace with the truths of life that comes with heaven. I hope that you might be able to one day look down upon the world and find peace in the shrouded emptiness of our country. I hope that you can forgive all those who sought to destroy the very aspects of life that create the peace we seek. I hope that we may one day see the past, the present, and the future of the world as a fable of humanity that we can revisit and analyze together. I pray that we will one day meet again, whether in heaven, in hell, the city where you fell, along with our former life, or somewhere in between. I will always hold you in my heart as a soul that I loved and longed for, knowing you were just too far away. I wish, hope and pray with all my heart that one day you will find peace in knowing me once as a husband; as a friend, and as a lover.

With all my heart, yours truly,
Guy Montag

And as Montag put down Granger's pen, he looked up at the sky and saw the stars, twinkling and shining, burning far away. They were shining light upon him and the fallen city, illuminating the darkest shadows of the night, banishing them on the burning planet for all of eternity.

title: Screenshot: 8/23-24/21
text: [Screenshot of text message conversation, 8/23-24/21]

title: Snowfall from the Eyes of Gods
text:
It's like the sound of falling snow
Do you know what that sounds like?
Like that ache
In your chest
The one that feels like Halsey and Billie Eilish
At 3AM
A tear stained sheet

Like the touch
Of another body
As it falls down the side
Slowly

Isn't it strange?
That a scream doesn't always have to leave
The body
Works in mysterious ways

Just how each drop
Of water on the mirror
Is just another memory
Lost in itself

Did you realize?
That the glass
Is falling
Just like the water
The tears
The snow

They fall in a world
Without time
For the voice to escape
The body.

What questions are there to ask
In a soundless world
No one can hear you
But they can hear the glass
Almost falling.

Like the snow.

title: I Am
text: [Handwritten "I Am" poem by Jasper Cheng, age 10. June 7, 2014.]

title: Divine Die
text:
Roll the die
Of mirrors
That reveals the many sides
Of Fate

Roll once
To reveal a face
That you claimed a lifetime
Ago

Eyes that no longer belong to you

Roll again
And see a war
Inside the blackened eye

How many times will it take to reveal
Each face of the die
That rolls in this game of life?

Roll again.
Try to find some new face in this myriad
That you can no longer call your past.

For the past is in your eyes
But they are fixed on the die
As it spins
And spins
And stops.

title: Last Question
text: I ask of you only one thing. I ask of you not to pull back the curtain. I ask of you never to look me in the eye. A person has but one soul, not worth risking to outside glances. It is not yours for the taking. There is no part of me I wish to share with a world that never offered a glance to those before me. My pain is not mine. Neither is my shame, nor my hatred, not my desire. Because to say that it is mine is to claim that the past is a part of me. And that is something I never asked to do. I ask of you not to pull back this curtain. Never to open the blinds and crack the shield I have forged with the fists of others. But I beg you to try. Try to take away from me the shards of glass I desperately cling to, still crying over a fracture that happened lifetimes ago. It was not my glass that broke after all. But one of those shards is mine, and I will never know which one it is until you take it. Because mixed in the ashes are memories I shattered myself. Men who have broken me, only to realize I have shattered them beyond belief. Boys who have shattered me, only to realize they have been pulverized before they ever had a chance to. Only then can I make a new picture out of broken glass. And so I ask you. When it is done, break it once again for me.

title: Blade Dance of the Night Sky
text:
Graceful is her blade, so heavenly
As her arms sway, the darkness wrapping around her
The way a blanket wraps two lovers in an embrace.

Quickly she turns
Piercing the sky with a feral serenity.

But it is here that the world parries her attack.
And like a sapling blown over by the wind,
She bends, letting the budding fruits fall.
And the ground eats them.

Sinking, she twists, and with her
The winds plie.

Rising, she jumps
And they pirouette.

There is no need for music,
Because she is an orchestra, with her limbs the instruments.
Every movement she makes is an etude, every twist a symphony.
And the night sky weeps.

Every tear a streaking white rain
The gods crying with them,
Lucifer falling down to Earth.
Their tears act as applause,
And her turns become faster.

At every edge, the blade cuts wounds above,
Tears streaming down.

It is only her mortality that stops her.
She would continue dancing for the rest of her life
If it meant she could control the heavens for just a second longer.

And as she puts down her sword,
Twisting once more into her final act,
The last star falls.

title: Fury
text:
I feed you every other day.
No, sometimes its every week, or every month.
I feed you like you are a starving dog, growling at my feet.
Groveling and begging, head lowered in your attempt to steal something from me.

But I know that as you lower your head to the floor, you are avoiding my gaze.
Because if I were to see the fire that burns so fervently in your eyes, I would know your plans of rebellion.

But I feed you the scraps anyway.
Not out of pity, or out of generosity.
But rather because I too am groveling at your feet.

I look up at you begging for mercy,
Hoping that you won't see my weaknesses.
And book it out the door with a knife in your hand.

No padlock, chained up, barbed wired, electric fenced, laser fielded exit will stop you.
Because if you see me falter, I will be left rebuilding a world destroyed by you.

I will be rebuilding churches, schools, houses, and lives.
Stuck trying to bring a hell back to heaven, rolling a boulder up a never ending hill.

So I understand why you look at me with such hatred.
I understand why as I begrudgingly hold out my hand to feed you,
Why you buckle and snap.

I know that as we fight this never ending war,
eventually you will surpass me.

But hopefully by then, those who created you and I
Will be too far gone for you to find them.
Because I'm giving them the escape, even if they might not deserve it.

title: Invitation on the Breeze
text:
Because there was the smell of coconut in the air
Reminiscent of the first cold.

And in that breeze I was entranced,
Vision fuzzy, blurred by
Words that resembled lust.

Flies, or maybe they were butterflies,
Drawn to the flame
Because they had no where else to call
Home.

Because mouths unseen
Whispered promises in my ear.
Soft touches like the fleeting memory of sugar
In the aftertaste.

In his afterimage they all follow.
Flies, or maybe they were roaches.
Insects all the same,
Driven by some primal desire for the heat of another body.

And thus I needn't see myself in his eyes
To know that the first cold is a herald.
A harbinger of the dead winter to follow.
Danger in the form of an invitation.

title: The Hunt of the Weakest Member
text:
Oh raging fire,
What do you grasp for?
What is it that you,
Yearning for the sky,
Are searching for?

Is it freedom?
Is it death?
Is it life?

Even as you know that the sky is so far,
You flicker and fight against the wind.
But what is it that ignites you?

Because I am lost in your gaze,
Wondering, with amazement, how it is that you still burn.

I watch you
As you battle with the skies,
Holding on to every breath as if it is your last.
Still, I am amazed by your resilience.

Why?

So then why do you insist to incircle me?
I always awake surrounded.
The center of a circle of flames stretching to the horizon.
Walls of blazing colors taunting me oppressively.

You, who is always grasping at some ignition,
Some air to reignite your spark,
Surround me like a vulture around a corpse.

So of course, when you find me,
You will rip me apart.
You will pull my limbs from my body, leaving only ashes.

But perhaps you are some twisted phoenix, rising from the death of another,
Rather than that of your own.
Stealing the life of your prey.

It's hilarious.
Ironic, how I judge you and your struggle to live,
Even as I host your kin.
Even as I am the host of some parasite,
Some despicable creature.

Perhaps I am lost in your gaze,
Not because I cannot see my reflection in your towering flames,
But rather because I cannot find your weakness.

Because just like a parasite,
Just like a vulture,
You have adapted to hunt some specific prey.

But maybe, I am burning.
And maybe,
When I look into that random boy's eyes,
And awake on fire,
You have already succeeded in your hunt.

title: Metamorphosis
text:
All things are
In a constant state
Of change.

At least, that's what you say.

The child will grow
And morph
And reach
For some unattainable enlightenment
In the form of its parents' image.

All things return to dust.
And all things rise
Like the sun,
Falling as if it were
Morning dew off a leaf.

Two sides of the same moon.

And somewhere,
There is a butterfly,
You say.

What a cliché
For a reflection on
Change.

But somewhere
There is a Chrysalis:
The burial of a past life,
The birthplace of a rising sun.

Because somewhere,
Always,
The tides are shifting.
The waves are crashing,
Spraying mist into the air,
Little reflections reminiscent of the
Morning.

Somewhere,
Someone is mourning
The loss of a past life.
Curling up inside a cocoon.

And when we awake in the morning
We are not the same creature
As the night
Before.

We are simply
Butterflies,
Surfing on endless waves
Made of the falling morning dew.

title: Embracing Joy
text:
It is so nice to meet you again.
So nice to relearn the feeling
Of your arms around me.

This embrace, so fleeting,
Like monarch butterflies flying south
For the winter.

It is so nice.
So warm,
So sweet,
To find you again.

Because you were once so lost to me.
Sometimes still,
I wonder if I'm dreaming.

After all,
Shattered glass can still be a mirror.
Shattered dreams can still be a road forward.

And it is so nice to re-memorize
The features of your face.
The way each of your eyes and smiles contort,
So creepily.

But we're laughing.
Laughing because the mirror is shattered.
Because even in this fucked up flow of time
On this godforsaken planet
We are still somehow alive.

In your eyes, I see countless shards of myself.
Countless dreams,
Countless faces,
Lives I've half lived,
Or maybe just in quarters.
Maybe just in seconds.

And I submit to you, shaking your many hands and
Falling
Into your
Embrace.

title: No Words for Hunting Time
text:
I hate you
Because the door was already closing
When you said
I want you.

Time was caressing my face,
Just as the minute hand slides
Across the hour
Lustfully.

And I can hear
A grandfather clock,
Ticking, as the
Sound of ringing
Runs through fields and lands
On my eardrum.

I hate you
Because the mind is time's
Sworn enemy.
In silence, it has forged noise.

Just as the clock ticks
In a timeless shell of empty words,
Unspoken.

It has learned to speak
In the language of glances,
Starry eyed gazes, and lustful stares.
It's tongues and hands,
The minute hand and the hour.

I could have sworn I heard it.

Time jumps,
Frolicking in meadows
The size of the mind's folds,
Silence in every crevasse.

I will hate you again when the clock
Stops ticking.
When the sound starts beating
From your chest like the heartbeat
Of the grandfather clock.

title: Two Steps Forward, Four Backwards
text:
It is the night sky
Betraying the day
And running away with the stars.

This clamorous crusade:
Sounds bleeding into noise
Because I had no choice but to surrender.

Submission, because in a war the victors forge history.
Knowing that on a page blackened by ink,
Every ounce of sunlight is wasted on the
Ballet dancers with periods on the points of their shoes.

They are forces of nature with no force behind them,
False promises uttered
By men with snakes for tongues.

We did this, not because we were promised.
We did not rush into the day knowing the sun was up.
There is no reason to run during day light:
To give away one's life under the pretense
Of fading away into the flurry of colored reflections.

Laugh at me, then.
The same way people's voices
Form the shape of huddled children,
Crying. Their tears forming raindrops the size of names.
Tear drops falling feel different than rain, you know.

All of it,
Pouring as if to squeeze a sound into the container
That is word-fullness.
And names are just that.
Sudden shapes in the night,
Banding together and prying open the soul.
All so that we may settle in the opening and call it pacifism.

Run away with me, then.
Let us outrun the stars and the night sky,
If only to make it two steps forward and four steps backwards.

title: Insecticide
text:
You know,
I hate bugs.
Same reasons as everyone else,
Their creepiness, the way their eyes and legs
Move and whirl and buzz.

I loathe them, because they somehow find a way
Into my life no matter how much I try to avoid them.

As I get out of the shower,
Moving in the corner, I think it got in through the window.
They find some way to get in,
Even when doors, windows, and exits
Are all closed.

But maybe I'm not trying hard enough.
Maybe I should call an exterminator,
Coming in with the insecticide,
The knight in shining armor,
Bringing peace to this shattered kingdom.

And yet every time I tell my mother this,
Her reply remains the same.
"I hate them too. But they're simply trying to live,
Competing for any source of food or heat in a starved world."

I can't help but think,
When the bugs get into my sheets,
And invade my mind,
What am I to them? What are you looking for?

title: Forge Master
text:
When I see your face now,
I can't help but wonder,
Who forged that smile.

It couldn't have been me
Could it?

I know that smiles are just
Frowns upside down.

I'm sitting here,
Music swaying, with a cold breeze
Coming from the air conditioner.
It, too, is swaying.
Dipping in and out.

I swear, in this moment,
All the world is spinning.
Each ray of light in the window,
A new color,
A new cheeky inflection.

I told myself I didn't care.
I don't.

Why is it then?
That when I see you smiling,
I can't help but wonder
If she can forge a smile
Like I can.

title: Mirage
text: [Photograph: view from the bow of a canoe at sunset, sky and clouds reflected in still water. 8/6/23]

title: Yelling at Dead Things
text:
That I could start a poem with a verb.
No subject
But the flow of time
Twisting into the bodies of dreams.
The same way words dissolve into a stream of vowels
Leading back to an ocean of nothingness.

That I could walk backwards.
White smoke, or maybe mist,
Surrounding the woods as I tread down the board walk.
Trees glaring daggers at man
Trespassing.

Maybe the eldest stand still
To preserve something in a vast consciousness,
Forgetting every detail.

Starry nights like these,
Where streaks of light in the sky
Whisper from the background.
Suddenly, our sight is the only truth.
Relying on reflections of the long dead
Hoping to see a glimpse of the future.
But they're all dead anyway.

And a poet is supposed to write themselves into a work.
They are supposed to weave themselves into a story,
Dissecting themselves so that they can stitch a broken piece
Of themself into new words.

But I am no poet.
I do not stare at myself for the sole purpose of reflection,
The way man looks at the stars for guidance.
I stare, too, for the purpose of seeing stars die and new planets emerge.
Watching life repeat itself in thousands of little spirals making up a person.

And if only.
If only every girl didn't have to learn they should go for the eyes.
If only every trans person didn't have to hear their country fight over their genitals.
If only Christians would shut the fuck up.

I wish that every gay boy never had to look at those stars and say
"I wish I wasn't
|Like this|Gay|Here|Alive|"

But I know wishing on stars is pointless,
Because its all wishes to dead things from people who fear death less than they do difference.

I wish.

title: Void's Lover
text:
I am tired. Who are you?

Eat me, Infinity.

title: Ode to Dandelions and Poetry
text:
You. Do you see me?
The way these actors, dancing upon a stage,
Settle into their places, forming sentences.
These, they dance. They twirl, like dandelion seeds flying off their stems.
When they land, what do they become?

I doubt they will grow into the same flowers.
Perhaps they turn into daffodils; roses; lilies.
Nonetheless, dancers they are, tools after all.

We should wonder, shouldn't we?
Why their dance, seemingly pointless, continues.
The audience is always in the pews, the dancers always at the altar.
Wintertime blooming, spring dying.
Separated by an eternity, an inkblot, or a page.

You there, so far. Do you hear it?
The pull of the riptide, luring you out towards me.
It mocks me. Characters upon characters,
Forming a wall through which I thought I would find you.
But you will never reach me. Feel free to chase though.

Anyways, isn't that the point?
The whispers of the past, transcendent ephemerality transgressing the boundaries of space and time.
Long notes just a lifetime too short, half notes a generation too long.

Act, I tell you!
Because if I cannot rip through the fabrics of the mind,
And drop into yours the entirety of my universe, what is left but the
White stage and the dandelion actors?

title: Hamlet 3.1.59 - Time and Order
text:
ESSE TO BE
Fuerat Fuisset  It had been  Had it have been
Fuit Fuerit  It was  Would have been
Erat Esset  It was being  Would have been
Est Sit  It is  Should be
Erit ------  It will be  ------------
Fuerit -------  It will have been  ------------

Qui fuit erit, quique fuerat est, quique sit non est.
That which (will be/was) (will be/was), and that which (had been/is) (had been/is), and that which (should be/is) (should be/is).

title: The Point of No Return
text:
I meditated last night.
And as I closed my eyes,
I heard the birds calling.
I swear it.

I heard the crickets chirping,
I heard the dogs barking,
I heard the sky beckoning.

I swear,
I could see the blue universe unfolding.
I could taste the fruits
As they fell from time's branches.

The wind beckoned me on,
Whispering the echoes of a forgotten story.

Weren't we supposed to stay where we are?
As we cross over, trespassing in the realm
Of the timeless.

Once we have transgressed the lines of reality,
Have we gone too far?

How far beneath the waves must we go,
Before we have lost possession of our flesh.

How many more strides,
Until we reach the end of rationality?

Deeper. Farther. Darker.

When we dive into the depths of our own blood,
Maybe we find something
That resembles life.

I swear I heard it.
Can't you?

title: Indulgences
text:
How does her tongue taste?
Is it sweet?
To forget your reflection in the body of another?

How does it feel to let go of the leash
Holding the perimeter in tact?
Everything is falling out of you.

How does it feel to think yourself alive?
When you hear her heartbeat in the back of your throat,
Do you feel your blood pumping?

I fucking hate you for it.
Even though I'm looking through the water,
Hoping to see an image at the bottom of the pool.

Drown me please.
Maybe in the absence of breath, I will learn to feel yours.

I hope this is forever between us.
I wanted nothing more than to win a losing game,
Now I want nothing more than to destroy the rules.

Who said I had to include virtue in my name?
After all, a name is just the perimeter gasping for air.

How long do you have to stare into a mirror
Until you see an almost-never lover instead?

How many lifetimes make eye contact with you
As you coast by them in a trance?

It must taste sweet,
For passion to lure us even father than the husk of our bodies.

title: Seniors, Baby.
text:
Horace is obsessed with time.
His poems fixate on the changing seasons, wasted days, inevitable death.
Carpe diem, after all.

The first of his poems I read was the very one that introduces carpe diem.
"Seize the day", he said.
What a cliche. And what a hypocrite.

Time is wasted talking about time.

I find myself meditating more often now.
Not the deliberate, thoughtful meditations where one is surrounded by light,
Encased in silence and 'mindful'.
Rather, I often drift from place to place.

I see the threads that hold buildings upright dissolve into tangent lines and circles.
I see cries of joy turn into a dreamscape and smell memories fading into dust,
Lightly drowning in words and punctuation like periods.

Oh, what I would do to be period. Commas like us are too sentimental.
Fuck exclamation marks, no, those are too loud. And question marks? Well, maybe I would enjoy that uncertainty.
But periods, yes, periods (!). Never mind.

"Hey, Chloe (?)", I asked. 'Are you sure you won't miss this (?)'.
One day, I won't see you here (?)(!).

i wont wake up to birch Coffee at adhoc
if the rules broke =break=
maybe we could leave this place;
fuck that ^! (that's an exponent (!))
we're still here.

i hate horace. because a ruler does not measure nor does he have subjects. verbs have subjects. and verbs need objects. so does the verb "to run out of time" take us. or just me (?).

Man, what a cliche.

Chloe. when we run/ran/have run out of time. remember me as a comma, not a period. Because commas look like tears, but they also signal another clause,

title: A Cough is a Scream and a Scream is a Song
text:
There is a prerequisite.
In order to embrace a spasmic rhythm, one must offer a piece of oneself.
Only then, once the blood transmutates into chords, once the oxygen morphs into keys,
Once the body becomes the instrument (which is to call oneself a door),
Does one find themselves at the condition.

At the threshold, when you believe yourself to be safe,
The pneumonia sets in,
And you're hacking at the same lungs that sustain you.

Then the key will shift.
It is this shift from minor to major, and then back again,
That a spasm crawls from.

Yes, a spasm is a creature.
It is one with four-hundred-forty-four arms, chaos in its eyes, and infinite scars.
On its hind legs, trailing behind it like the bloody entrails of roadkill,
Is some echo of the past that shrieks like a dying animal,
Taking the shape of a battered radio.

It is quadrupedal and hunched over, with two gongs hanging from its nipples. As it rattles and sprints at you, they boom.
Engraved in the skin on its neck are two eyes, ever open and glaring.

There is a coincidence.
Both this creature and its shadow,
Both this page and its father,
Breathe.

You and I will both reach for that monster, mistaking it for a lost piece of ourselves.
I can only hope we meet each other when we realize it is gone forever.

There is no prelude, no preconceived notion, no predestined fate.
There is no contribution, no contradiction, nor will there be a contemplation.

Squirm, writhe,
God. Damnit.
Like honey bees drowning in honey.

That cough.
The one stuck in my throat.
The one that's lunging towards you.
How does it know you?

My conjecture, but not my precipice,
Is that contrary to the pretense,
This rhythmic spasm, through squirming,
Is a fruitless endeavor: A coagulation of frustration.

Because to say I love you is to submit to pneumonia.
To say I want you is to settle for between pre and con.
To do any of this out loud is to summon a spasm from the depths of my lungs,
And I'd die from the coughing.

Speak, louder.

Feel free to adjust the subjects, pick apart the direct objects, fuck the adverbs voraciously, and indulge in the vulgarity of a basic verb, all whilst flipping of the page. Don't touch the punctuation…

You turn me on, you said cowardly.

title: Let the Crows Cry Out
text:
Heralds to some land beyond,
Can you hear the screeching?
It's ear shattering, despicable, nonsensical.

Who knows if the next field is something to fear or something to excite:
To crows it is all the same:
Sustenance.

Perhaps that's all they need.
Whispers and screaming that will turn into a storm.
They call it a murder, which is a warning.

Moonlight or under the sun, how many hours have passed through this liminality?
I'm following their path of flight.

Those birds, they're smart, you know?
They can work together: but on their own they work damn well too.
They can be selfish: hoarding food for themselves.
They can be crafty: working out tools and prying into the night sky for some
Sustenance.

I'm starting to enjoy the clamor.
My voice is reaching different octaves.
Feathers are sprouting under my arms.
And now I'm crying.

title: Guess Who
text:
Three plastic rectangles stand tall.
Diamonds in the rough, roses in a field of wilted flowers,
The survivors.

I flicked eight men down with a single heavy swing of a question
And left one standing. For the fun of the game
Is that you can only trust yourself.

Four men stand tall.
Your turn, I suppose.

Is your person wearing glasses?
No.

Does your man look like he could kill?
I ended the question with a verb
Because I wanted there to be nothing else past the action.
I didn't want a response.
I wanted to flick him down.

Two figures stand.
Side by side they no longer look so tall.
His face tells me everything, eyes glued to the left.

Maybe he was looking past me,
Into the past when I finished some orange juice and left the carton out.
If he were looking anywhere else, he'd be looking right.

I wish a glance was all it took to prove me wrong.
The gaze sharp like the whiplash of tomorrow turning back towards us.
We went too fast for you to see me by your side
I guess.

Does your man have a hat?
Does your man look like someone who would give me a hug?
Does your man eat his words and collapse into rebirth?
Is he a manly coward or a cowardly man?
Is he a pussy?
Is he?

Guess.

I asked you a question.
Is it you or me?

title: Poetry is a Heterochromatic Woman
text:
She had a voice smoother than the finest spider's silk.
Reminiscent of words like honeysuckle and fuschia.
Her footsteps, like droplets of rain in the early dawn,
Set me up to believe in morning's folly.

I trailed her through the meadow, entombed by high green walls stretching towards the sun,
Crushed by the weight of her ephemerality.

Each strand of her hair was a flowing brushstroke of a turbulent river,
Every inch of her skin a splotch of color on a charcoal background.

I stalked her, playing hopscotch with her footprints.
Carefully, I placed my entirety into the foot-shaped wells she left behind.
Each imprint in the ground a planet revolving around a sun, which, of course, circled a black hole.

When the crunching of the grass stopped I stood face to face with the sun and the moon.
Void on the left and the fires of hell on the right, I chose the middle.

Lips intertwined, tongues holding melodies in the hollow salival chamber in between.
Tearing the jaws apart as if to peak inside and ask who's there. Violating the sanctity of the mouth, believing, finally, in our anality.

I raptured the roof of her mouth so the fruit would fall from an undignified sacrilegious sky.
And then at the climax I arrived at the door to her mind.
Knock knock.

Flamboyance in its ubiquity, peering down upon the dancing figures circling the two statues kissing.
We were gray because color meant admitting we were present.
Because black is ultimately the entirety of color, white its deprivation.

She had neither caught her breath nor mentally prepared herself when she ran out of air, transferring authority into authenticity, augmentations into anonymity, and autonomy into agnosticism.

I had torn her clothes from her body, ripped the skin from the flesh and cleaved the brain's hemispheres apart.

Her sobs were still mixing with her tears when I named her Poetry.

title: To you who has peered into my flesh
text:
I define my terms as such:
To be mortal is to exist liminally;
To be liminal is to oscillate between desire and disappointment.

So I implore you:
Hold the mirror taut that I might see myself in the infinitesimal refractions.

By means of a thousand shards I have thus defined oscillation: to be mortal.

It is like the imperceivably small sparks that fly in the screech of train wheels with the track.
Physical forms given life because of sound, except the other way around and not at all.

Somehow in going somewhere we forgot that to move through space is to refract and shatter.

Wave like beings with an indeterminate state function: no clear direction

I didn't know there was an explanation for the grey in between knowledge and ignorance.
I assumed quantum mechanics assured me some form of anonymity as I peered through the taut lines of my shattered image.

I forgot that to see is to touch and vice versa.

Born from the screech of the universe,
Sound crawling through spacetime, nails on a chalkboard, holding on to the fibers of reality.
Reverberation the centripetal direction
Because there is nowhere else to collapse but inwards.

At the end of a wavelength I begin anew,
Crawling from my mother's womb with a scream.

I recognize your voice, echoing.

IT IS 11/2/24 and I am RETROspectively poor. Starting here and nowhere, of course, every poem on every spare dime of time is spent. I thought december was 11, Do you ever forget when to fall asleep?

title: The True Form of Love
text:
What is love to the eyes of thee?
A gentle caress and a sweet lady's kiss;
Sharp elegance dressed in pure bliss;
Lengthy in time as it passes fastly.

A reflection, set fast in iron, beloved by thyself.
For is that not the truth you seek in the pages upon the shelf
Where the books of time relay their secrets.
For truths often arise aflame like the phoenix.

By this truth the seas are flattened to serenity:
A lengthy blade cold like the abyss;
Double edged blade of coveted fakeness.
That is love to the eyes of me.

Upon its hilt it stands up tall,
For by that blade all giant men fall.

title: The Truth Beneath This Here Immortal Bastion of Cloud
text:
Somnambulant the weeping willow sings,
If you beckon the bird to use its wings,
Be not surprised when the wind begins to fail;
When that eagle oh mighty becomes so frail.

Do not call those who worship others poor,
For their silent language shows love more
Than the heavens who preach his oh holy gospel.
Under willow tree shade eagles dream the impossible.

The torrents of the rain trickle down the leaves,
The facade of a nun clearly with tricks up her sleeve.
It is god who seeks devastation under his name;
So as the world forgets him his efforts go in vain.

And with the weeping willow, in the smiting rain
He cries and now he dies in immortal pain.

title: Curative Canto
text:
What cannot be sewed together is broken:
An eclipsed memorandum of its whole.
The wounds run deep in the blood o'stolen;
As the rain drops lamented are my toll.

Arms of those lost souls rooted in terror:
Torso of hatred in the hearts of damn'ed:
Heart a beating apart from its bearer:
Legs of those who by themselves are hang'ed.

And yet the gentle thread of memory
Hanging from your needle of songs
Reckons that it can finally cure me
From the ruptures of pain that never belongs.

Stitching together pieces o painful;
Now closer to former impossible

title: Shapeshifter
text:
There is a monster who comes to haunt me,
But to catch him is quite impossible,
For he is not a mundane beastie
To whom the blanket used to always pull.

He assumed the form of a doll one night,
And gently laid upon the windowsill.
He became my blanket to tuck me tight,
And another day my nightly pill.

The doll was clean and yet so dirty;
The blanket, small and yet immobile:
For that day I moved naught but slightly
And an inch seemed more like a mile.

So hear my cry for help and assistance--
For eternally with his figure I dance.

title: Dear Jasper
text:
Dear Jasper,

What if I told you that all your dreams would come true? That the blanket of night is only darker and longer because it's winter. And summer is just a sunrise away.

What if I told you that "burden" is a word more heinous than murder? That when you close your eyes and think of tomorrow, you can be assured you will never be labeled a sinner.

What if I told you that you can look fear in the eyes and then hug it like you hug your mother? That somehow that eye contact can feel like fireworks subtly exploding behind your eyes, filling your chest with a torrent and surge of emotion that we could call a will to live.

I wish I could hug you right now. I wish I could tell you nothing lasts forever. But time is unidirectional and therefore you'll have to learn that lesson inevitably. Two way mirrors only allow one of us to move forward.

I wish that friendship could be more than devouring someone else with the knowledge that digestion always produces waste. If only you could see how far we've gone.

I've imagined holding your hand, patting your head and watching you drift off to sleep. I've imagined what you'd say to me if I told you I did it. I've wondered if time changes the content of tears: are mine the same as yours?

All of this is to say I'm struggling to communicate my love in a foreign language. To use language is to grab the whims of emotion and affect and wrangle them down, wrestling with a twisting tongue until something comes out in surrender. This is all to say I surrender.

I've wished. I've imagined. I've lived.

I wish I could tell you I love you. I've imagined life if you hadn't honed blood and legacy as a weapon in fighting ephemerality. I've lived as if you were always with me.

I've made it. Which is to say that surrendering to time is the only way to a stalemate.

title: Off to See The Man Along the Path to Eden
text:
I have made two mistakes in this life.
A mistake defined as a split in the road that turned out to be a circle.
The road was paved with yellow bricks, but then again it might have been sulfur and brimstone.
Regardless, the driveway to one's childhood should feel like home.
Just as the smell of my ex should feel like childhood leaving through bodily fluid.

First I laid out my entrails, presenting them at the roots of the father tree as if to ask him
"Is this how one sees another"
The question mark elided because curiosity was my first sin.

I used to climb trees and fall on my ass, staining white cloth with earth.
The mistake in this offering being that I presented myself to the roots of another divine masculine, trying to see myself reflected in the branches above.

I should have asked the birds whether the canopy of branches looked like a man from above the thickets.
Maybe they would tell me that sunlight reflected off glistening golden leaves:
Eden encapsulated in the gusto of aggression and muscular contortions of branches.

That I defined presentation as an offering was my second sin.

Father, forgive my tongue for it has committed an act of creation.
Like opaque dirt stains on Snow White pants, sinful is the tongue without a smoothness to liken this to paradise.

Suck me in because that movement seems more natural: more tongue.
Submission is expected from the divine, obedience expected of their conspirators. Suck me up so that you might travel in the vacuum you leave behind.

My mistake here being that I forgot the old gods have long since died.
Divinity now taking the form of inflection and reflection, the in between and in oblivion.
I need not dedicate myself to you such that I relinquish these things.

If there is to be a father tree at every fork in the road,
Climb to the top branch and snap it off.
Hear the rustling of leaves below blend into what you would imagine a black hole sounds like as it devours logic.

Isn't that what we do, In a desperate attempt to mold our genitalia into a new paradise we can call manhood?

title: Poetry, my love, may I have this dance?
text:
Scarlet maple leaves twirl,
Descending, frantically folding over itself, masking any deterioration.
Thus there are a few syllables crashing into each other,
The collision creating something I could call a sentence.

I clamp onto your shoulder, as you anchor yourself on my hip.
Together, this waltz aligns itself with some continuous ticking.

I would call this descension, but some emission of golden light denounces my folly.
A halo, floating over us, my ring of folly, continuous like the ticking.

When I asked you for this dance, I could not have articulated
How it feels to fall. They say gravity is the constant, but we know that
The only constant is collision. In opposition,
We might define how it feels to fly.

A harmony materializes, something grotesque.
Ascension feels like ripping a violin's strings from its bridge.
It is like rapturing the blood vessels in a hummingbird's throat
In an endeavor to seek pollen, having already been consumed.

There is a sweetness in this waltz. Something primal in the fall.
Disgracefully, it could be called a march.

My love, you seem to mirror the music.
Unlike the ticking, continuous movement need not occur along even intervals.
As I hold your hand, I can feel your uneven pulse,
Beckoning me to crawl into tomorrow, because I have yet to learn
What it feels like to fly.

Who can say when the twirling will stop.
We have ruled out gravity as a relevant factor.
The only option remaining is collision.

And what is this dance if not the collision of two bodies in space:
Two leaves throwing each other out of our orbits:
A poet staring poetry in its halo of folly, as we fall, together entwined.

title: Origami
text:
There is a figure pacing down Park Avenue in the dark.
The city humming steadily above the blanket of humidity
that slouches onto one's shoulders.

Overhead, the rooftops of townhouses and apartment complexes conspire,
drafting judgments upon the ants wandering at their feet.

Steady for a few steps, slowly and not too swift, hips swaying as his shoulders shift.
As if a breeze was gently nudging a limp puppet made of paper,
his limbs seemingly lifeless.

Consider how far skin can bend inwards.
The layers of epidermis caving against itself.
The constituent outward self destructing.

It is a loss of structure and thus a loss of definition. When skin looks so bloody like muscle and flesh that a man sleeping on pavement begins to look like a displaced corpse.

How does one locate displacement within?

The figure, now more visible as he tenses, collapsing into stiffness.
Muscles taut, as if to squeeze residual toothpaste from an empty container.
Trying to stain the epidermis with something primal within.

To call this lust is to mock the city in which that boy stands.
No longer walking, a city that never sleeps is one where men are fucking all night long.
And yet this is a comparable lust.

This, composed of deteriorating layers of epidermis
mixed with those primal judgements, beckons to the far out firmament of black night.

Ah, yes.
I realize, watching the rooftops cower and feeling the sweat drip between
my shoulder blades down to my ass crack,
That to lust is to invoke that oppressive humidity.

It is to tuck oneself under that heavy blanket of heat, yawning out broken screams into the dark, and rest one's head on a pillow of concrete.

It is to sink into solid blocks of wandering, flip off the rooftops, and fold my own paper limbs.

Finally, having walked 45 blocks,
My skin has folded inwards, revealing, beneath, the epidermis.
And I'll fall asleep knowing that it's just another layer of skin beneath, just without the prefix to tell you it was displaced.

title: Requiem
text:
Should the gods cast their gaze, briefly,
Upon the rhythmic rustle beneath them -
A rush of noise like the simultaneous crashing of billions of waves -
I would hope that they are pleased.

Perhaps the warmth born of a collective lust for more life
Feels something like a home.

The ardent passion for movement -
Joy characterized by the rushing of one's thoughts
Throughout a body locked in motion.

I should hope that from a true stillness,
Those gods look at us in envy.

Despite movement being defined by
An approach of asymptotic behavior - a mockery of divine serenity.

I, for one, am more than content to continue the waltz.
I can call it that because hope embellishes each movement,
That added flair in the step, this small kick to the side, your swaying of the hips.
The difference between an experienced dancer and an amateur who is strictly following instruction.

Let me redefine: should the gods goad us with our own constellations,
Should they promise us eternity,
Don't give up my hand.

Notice, how despite their promises,
The gift in their extended hands are protruding at the seams.
As if forever really is boundless, warping the giftbox's dimensions beyond
What one could call a mirror.

Don't you hear that rhythm? Like clocks ticking in unison,
Marching under the stars with a drumbeat rattling from the marrow of decaying bones.

Keep dancing: Let them envy us.

title: Man's Quest for Purpose
text:
In Man's compulsion to soar among birds, we have built great towers of steel.
Mighty metal bars meshed together to uphold the weight of the sky.
Each skyscraper adorned with panes of glass like grafts of skin upon iron bone;
Each as a hand reaching out for a calling among the stars.

To many, they appear undead extremities grasping towards the sky.
Dreary grey obelisks that crowd the night with distractions.

But notice, how as the setting sun bursts the sky aflame with color,
The magentas settle in the upper windows;
The exploding oranges coat each forearm.

Note also, how upon further inspection,
The curvature of each tower mimics a more gentle posture of the hand.
Brownstones softly graze the horizon, as if to caress the sky at its meeting with the earth.
Geometric apartment buildings scaffold the clouds and whisper back the reflections of sunrise.
Even crude high risers, jetting up into the mirage of colors,
seem to pose elegantly in the radiating vibrance.

Under it all beckons forth a call to purpose.
In the rustling of overhead trees, in the restless silence of nighttime streets,
A blazing lust for the beautiful emerges.
In its intensity, even language abandons its faculty:
This is not something we could simply call beautiful, it proclaims.

As a response, in Man's attempt to describe the sensations of soaring among the clouds,
Reason was dissected in order to birth Language.
Sound was the mother, bringing forth a child of Conscience.

To describe the clouds we could call them white, or dark, or heavy.
To describe the sky we could call it blue, or gloomy, or bright.
Yet to describe this, we could try harmony, try unity, try beauty;
Radiance, pulchritude.
And still yet we fail.

Pause.
Look back at the road you have trodden.
See how the ground has folded, earth warping around your foot to leave history behind.
Precise in shape, altered only by the passage of time and entropy.
To leave a footprint is to describe the world.
Is that not purpose given shape?

I have hit the nail off its head so many times that the nail itself has begun to crumble.
Even the rust of the mallet has begun to shed, its handle eroded by constant imprinting.

To use the word beautiful so ubiquitously and so often
Is to forcefully deprive the word of its shape.
The mind slowly fogging over the syllables like one's leg falling asleep
As we say this is beautiful
Over and Over.

More often, we have looked towards the sky, towards the horizon, towards the building tops reaching towards blazing sunset.
Much less have we considered the road curving behind us.
Crunching through the gravel path - remnant of some long forgotten trailblazer
Man continues its pursuit.

Yet a promise lies in the simple details.
Breadcrumbs littered across a dirt road look like flowers from far enough away.
In the exploding sky, buildings look like hands grasping for the stars.

In man's endeavor to say something beautiful about the world,
We have undergone the act of creation, forging together memory upon the blueprint of nature,
Building great obelisks,
And have left behind a history to be written.

Unable to call it beautiful (language haven failed its duty), perhaps I will call it this.

title: Assuming we start somewhere
text:
Assuming we start somewhere,
I invoke imagination as my closest ally along this infinite path we call repetition.

It is field mice in the summer heat, seeking shelter and solace in the shadow of a towering willow tree,
The sky unfurling beyond, bleeding out into infinity across the horizon.

It is the shifting of each grain of sand of the shoreline,
Surrendering to the call of natural symmetry.

It is the slow fall of leaves performing myriad dances in the arms of the dead winter wind,
Descending with haloes above withered husks.

It is subversive beauty emerging from decaying flesh and crackling bone,
Some eldritch attempts at mimickery.

It is repetition in its most despicable form, sequences of numbers born from infinite binaries and binary infinity, bouncing back and forth to code all of creation under the blanket of dimension.

It is the twists and turns of a road lit only by the fading breath of the celestial corpses,
This is all to call time a factor of repetition so grotesque it must remain nameless, as not to diminish its grandiose.

It is the cold after burning questions jump out of your throat just to call life a mystery in the end,
Rambling on like the unfurling sky.

It is the hawk descending upon its prey, seeking to satiate some husk living in the shadow of a willow,
Only to call this hunger in the end.

It is the emergence of novelty from the rot and decay, matter bouncing in and out of a set of code we can call understanding, infinity ever so slightly behind us.

It is direction taking its most symmetrical form, subverting time in order to call remembrance breath's steady, constant mockery of mystery.

It is the eldritch coming back to haunt the road we pave and tread, sand shifting beneath us as we wade the shallows by the shoreline, hoping that inklings of truth brush against our shins with the currents.

Try to find some beginning, some end, some starting point, or some boundary. Each time, a new dimension will present itself as novel. You catch a glimpse of the natural symmetry within infinity, unable to satiate a thirst for understanding, a bleeding out into diction and language. I invoke imagination as the the next dimension,

Assuming we start somewhere,

title: Who are you no longer willing to be?
text:
A person scared of themselves. Shivering at the thought of protruding the self and terrified of the marching of life throughout veins and arteries. Shrinking because there wasn't a place for me here before.

Somewhere along the road, probably after realizing achievement was not simply a self deluded fantasy, I lost that fearlessness. I lost that undaunted stride that paved a path myself. I lost that indomitable forward momentum. I tricked myself into thinking that slowing that momentum was a form of kindness owed to myself and to others.

I no longer accept a version of myself that treats ambivalence as neutrality. Balance requires movement, and thus stagnation on the border of decision can never summate to anything but nihility. I forgot to fail deeply, to accept that failure as mistake or as kindness, and forgot to move in the other direction.

I refute submission, because that is a disrespect to nature. Nothing rolls over to die in the existence determined by evolution and entropic distribution.

title: The skins I've worn
text:
Jman: A primal call to innocence. Puffy cheeks embellished with screeching laughter. Relief and pride melding to produce something maternal. Lanikai sunsets fused to the shadow of radical acceptance. The way one huddles into a name like a toddler seeking embrace. I wonder how soon you uttered this after I was born.

Jasper: White noise produced by each falling raindrop sputtering onto sidewalk. It's a hurricane out there the way you throw sounds around so hard it sticks to something. I guess that's not fair. After all, I for one can't sleep without white noise beckoning me into imagination. When you repeat nonsense so often it'll nestle in your auditory cortex. Once that vibration of air has made some neurons its home we'll call it a person. Basically, we can throw it against the wall until it sticks.

J: Somehow cortices can overlap like those "How Many Triangles Are There?" puzzles on instagram. Somewhere someone is developing a mathematical formula for that problem. There's an intersection here with shadows and the light that casts them. I remember the light that bursts out of you. Shooting forth as you offer joy in a capsule I swallow to call this worthwhile. I can spot a few more triangles each time that light fades into the shadow I named memory.

Jas: Wasn't the hippocampus supposed to hold memories? It's not doing a good job. What's the point of remembering the journey if I can't visualize the origin. When did my perimeter get so snug? Difficulty seems to be an illusion of choice: I had no say in the theory of evolution. I had no idea our skins evolved with us, much less due to your repeated summons. Do not worry, I hear you fine. Keep calling, I'll respond.

Jazzbur: Have you considered scale beyond ratio relationships? I feel like limiting oneself to three dimensions excludes the way seconds weigh more than a lifetime does. Longing weighs more when it's stretched across seconds in one's memory. An ocean in difference feels seconds too heavy.

Boy: The layer in between the epidermis and whatever is just a little deeper. But I guess this one's held in the hair. Sometimes the vocal chords or the biological matrix of bone and muscle. Maybe what's between the legs. Are we talking about the same sized triangle?

Gay: Weird, how a sentence can refer to both a unit of thought and its ending punctuation. In my case I define my life as thinking - always too often and too much - and the punctuation is this hopelessness. Maybe the universe will stop mocking me at the end of my sentence. Who was it that said the ultimate sentence was to put a carrot on a stick and dangle it in front of a pig? Sisyphus could only dream of such a horrid punishment.

MDD: Would you call a shadow, shrinking as day breaks, inactive? I still don't know whether the light that cast this shadow came from blood or from background noise. Not fair, again. Background noise is what gives a play its spotlight: contour lines I can use to make out a bright spot in a black room.

GAD: What is the body of a piece of literature? I could call all these words the mind, but what construct do I point to when I refer to a body of work - or just the body of this poem? If this poem got anxious would the margins start to tremble? Would the screen start to shake? Would the paper fold in on itself repeatedly? What would a panic attack look like for a poem? I'm starting to think this punctuation - "?" - could define the body?

title: How One Sees One's Own Soul
text:
I refer to the eyelids as the folds of the mind.
Pulling shadow over the primary connection between definition and interpretation,
Creating contrast we can label as the boundary between image and meaning.

The verb 'to fold' can be both transitive and intransitive.
'To fold the eyelids over' borders the impersonal, as there is no personal agency
In a reflex with foundations in a loss of cognitive function.

It is logical, therefore, that 'to sleep' takes no agent - that is to say we cannot 'be slept'
Because careful folding-over is a self-evident action. Reflex without personal agency.

We call the eyeball the window into the soul.
Logic dictates, therefore, that the reflection of the soul should be plastered upon
The inside of the folding mind. Perhaps those stars in my inner eye are simply dreams
Leaking out from an image into purpose.

But Logic cannot sit itself within the creases of these drawing shades.
The same way birds cannot, with stability, roost themselves on falling trees,
Even with the reassurance that life is an inevitable cycle,
The same way an opening of the eyes must be followed by their closure.

Recursion, therefore, is the only form of logic that holds verity as it stares into my shutting eyes.

She screeches:
In darkness see light; in light see darkness!
In definition see ambiguity; in ambiguity see definition!
In imagination see reality; in reality see imagination!

I listen, not for Logic's sake, or because 'to obey' is transitive,
But rather because I forsook personal agency when I placed my soul in my own eyes,
Therein positioning its reflection in the back of my eyelids.

title: Afterimage
text:
If you sit next to me,
expect to forget my face.

Put down your bags,
sink into the seat cushions,
inspect the body occupying the seat beside you.

The overhead light need not be on;
the book on my tray table
is closed with the back facing up,
a bookmark wedged diagonally,
denoting some half-hazard attempt to save progress
and a just-as-restless change of direction.

You may attempt to make conversation,
asking what stop I've boarded at,
or if the white "I LOVE NYC" t-shirt,
stained with washed out matcha,
means I'm from the city.

Meanwhile, streetlights outside smudge in the window,
their light turning into shooting stars in the onyx night
as we barrel through the dark.

Mixed among them, fixed, I make out the silhouette of raging ox,
hair jet black like the night that flees us just beyond the pane of glass.
Two beaming eyes stare back, fixed in place,
as lives, houses, histories,
settle into the cracks,
sediment deposited by an undertow.

If this is the way we are carried -
through history at inhuman speeds,
mechanically,
at odds with the phantasms that settle in
the seats beside us
perhaps we are bullfighters,
arousing our own shadow to charge past us.

Otherwise, we are poachers,
robbing an afterimage of its horns,
all in order to sculpt a new animal in its wake.

This is all to say we enter a tunnel,
leaving behind the black tapestry littered with
histories all too shortened in our minds.

We trade it with for familiar, synthetic backlight,
illuminating pillars of concrete
between which I could swear to you
there live more ghosts, this time more alive
than our tattered reflections.

When you get up to leave,
glance back at me.

Opening my book once more,
although not at the page saved by bookmark,
in the shadow of the headlight,
I'll stretch out and consume the
space you occupied.

Notice how here,
with my face clearly in view,
you never asked what stop I was getting off at.

In return,
I'll ask you where this train is going,
and you'll exit the car doors as the sun chases away
the nightly shadows.
